Author

Lars Schilling

Dependencies

Show code 
working_dir <- "G:/Meine Ablage/Thesis/tvd_mi/"

paper_llm_score_name <- "tvd_mi_scores_paper_llm_review_1.parquet"

paper_human_name <- "tvd_mi_scores_paper_human_review_2.parquet"
Show code 
# Flags for skipped or invalid API responses
SKIP_MESSAGE <- "Skipped cause of invalid input"
TOTAL_FAILURE_MESSAGE = "LLM call failed after multiple retries"

AMOUNT_SETTINGS <- 112 # Set to expected amount of configuration setting
AMOUNT_PAPERS <- 98 # Set to expected amount of parsed papers

COMBO_COL <- c("paper_id", "model", "temperature", "reasoning_effort", "chain_of_thought", "scope") # Set to expected combo columns
CONFIG_COL <- c("model", "hyperparameter", "chain_of_thought", "scope") # Set to expected configuration columns
BASE_SCORE_COLS <- c(
  "tpr_tvd_mi_score_1", "tpr_tvd_mi_score_2", "tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score",
  "fpr_tvd_mi_score_1", "fpr_tvd_mi_score_2", "fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score"
) # Set to expected score columns
CLEAN_SCORE_COLS <- c(
  "TPR 1", "TPR 2", "TPR AVG", 
  "FPR 1", "FPR 2", "FPR AVG"
) # Set to wanted score labels
TOTAL_SCORE_COL <- "total_score" # Set to created total score column

# Bootstrap settings
NUM_ITERATIONS <- 1000
CONFIDENCE_LVL <- 0.95

# Posthoc-test settings
###! Holmes-correction was not possible. Should I switch to bonferroni, Tuckey or try another way to run post-hoc tests?
ALPHA <- 0.05
POST_HOC_CORRECTION = "bonferroni"
Show code 
load_pkg <- function(pkg) {
  if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
    library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
  }
}

load_pkg("arrow")
load_pkg("tidyverse")
load_pkg("lme4")
load_pkg("car")
load_pkg("emmeans")
load_pkg("multcomp")
load_pkg("multcompView")
load_pkg("broom.mixed")
load_pkg("transport")
load_pkg("knitr")
load_pkg("kableExtra")
load_pkg("cowplot")
load_pkg("ggtext")

Load data

Show code 
full_path_paper_llm <- file.path(working_dir, paper_llm_score_name)


paper_llm_score <- read_parquet(full_path_paper_llm,
                                as_data_frame = TRUE, mmap = TRUE)

full_path_paper_human <- file.path(working_dir, paper_human_name)

paper_human_score <- read_parquet(full_path_paper_human,
                                  as_data_frame = TRUE, mmap = TRUE)

Content checks

As multiple runs of APIs possibly created duplicate paper/configuration pairs, these have to be cleaned first. Besides that, the function creates metadata-columns which indicate weather combinations of paper and configuration have been attempted and yielded valid TPRs and FPRs.

Show code 
# Helper function to clean duplicates with granular success flags
clean_duplicates_function <- function(df, 
                                      combo_cols, 
                                      tpr_col, 
                                      fpr_col, 
                                      skip_msg = SKIP_MESSAGE, 
                                      fail_msg = TOTAL_FAILURE_MESSAGE) {
  
  df_cleaned <- df |>
    mutate(
      # Individual validity for the sub-metrics
      is_valid_tpr = !(.data[[tpr_col]] %in% c(skip_msg, fail_msg)),
      is_valid_fpr = !(.data[[fpr_col]] %in% c(skip_msg, fail_msg)),
      
      # The TVD-MI is only valid if BOTH are valid (Success of the TVD-MI score)
      is_valid = is_valid_tpr & is_valid_fpr,
      
      # Fail and skip flags for the compelete tier list
      is_failed  = (.data[[tpr_col]] %in% fail_msg | .data[[fpr_col]] %in% fail_msg),
      is_skipped = (.data[[tpr_col]] %in% skip_msg & .data[[fpr_col]] %in% skip_msg)
    ) |>
    mutate(
      quality_tier = case_when(
        is_valid   ~ 1, # Both TPR and FPR are clean
        is_failed  ~ 2, # Judge error
        is_skipped ~ 3, # Review generation error
        TRUE       ~ 4
      ),
      .orig_idx = row_number()
    ) |>
    group_by(pick(all_of(combo_cols))) |>
    arrange(quality_tier, .orig_idx, .by_group = TRUE) |>
    slice(1) |>
    ungroup() |>
    dplyr::select(-any_of(c(".orig_idx")))
  
  return(df_cleaned)
}
Show code 
paper_llm_score_cld <- clean_duplicates_function(paper_llm_score,
                                                 COMBO_COL,
                                                 "tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score",
                                                 "fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score")

paper_human_score_cld <- clean_duplicates_function(paper_human_score,
                                                  "paper_id",
                                                  "tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score",
                                                  "fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score")

Now the completeness of paper/configuration pairs can be checked.

LLM-authored reviews

The first table shows the amount of processed configurations per paper for all papers who have not been processed comepletly. The second table shows the amount of processed papers per configurations for all configurations who have not been processed comepletly. The third table is a summary of how many combination of paper and configuration have been processed and what success status they reached. The fourth table gives out the amount of processed papers and their success status for each configuration. The fifth table givous out the amount of processed configurations and their success status for each paper.

###! I feel like all of these tables are a bit to much. I would prefer to keep the summary only. What do you think?

Show code 
# Any incompletly processed papers?
paper_llm_score_cld |> 
  count(`Paper ID` = paper_id) |> 
  filter(n != AMOUNT_SETTINGS) |> 
  rename(`Amount of processed configurations` = n) |> 
  kable(format = "html", align = "r") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"))

Show code 
# Any incompletely processsed configurations?
paper_llm_score_cld |> 
  group_by(Model = model, Temperature = temperature, `Reasoning effort` = reasoning_effort,
           CoT = chain_of_thought, `Scope` = scope, ) |> 
  summarize(row_count = n(),
            .groups = "drop") |> 
  filter(row_count != AMOUNT_PAPERS) |>
  rename(`Amount of processed papers` = row_count) |> 
  kable(format = "html", align = "r") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"))
Model Temperature Reasoning effort CoT Scope Amount of processed papers
llama-3.3-70b-versatile 0 NA Explain your thought process step by step. full_text 22
Show code 
# Global
paper_llm_score_cld |>
  summarize(
    `Total amount of combinations` = n(),
    `Amount of valid results` = sum(is_valid),
    `Amount of failed results`  = sum(is_failed),
    `Amount of skipped results` = sum(is_skipped),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(`Success rate`= paste0(round((`Amount of valid results` / `Total amount of combinations`) * 100, 1), "%")) |>
  kable(format = "html", align = "r") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = F)
Total amount of combinations Amount of valid results Amount of failed results Amount of skipped results Success rate
8156 2143 5122 67 26.3%

# By configuration
paper_llm_score_cld |>
  group_by(model, temperature, reasoning_effort, chain_of_thought, scope) |>
  summarize(
    `Total amount of papers` = n(),
    `Amount of valid results` = sum(is_valid),
    `Amount of failed results`  = sum(is_failed),
    `Amount of skipped results` = sum(is_skipped),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(`Success rate` = paste0(round((`Amount of valid results` / `Total amount of papers`) * 100, 1), "%")) |>
  arrange(`Success rate`) |> 
  kable(format = "html", align = "r") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"),
                full_width = F)

Show code 
# By paper
paper_llm_score_cld |>
  group_by(`Paper ID` = paper_id) |>
  summarize(
    `Total amount of configurations` = n(),
    `Amount of valid results` = sum(is_valid),
    `Amount of failed results`  = sum(is_failed),
    `Amount of skipped results` = sum(is_skipped),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(`Success rate` = paste0(round((`Amount of valid results` / `Total amount of configurations`) * 100, 1), "%")) |>
  arrange(`Success rate`) |> 
  kable(format = "html", align = "r") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"),
                full_width = F)

Human-authored reviews

The first table shows which paper has been processed other than a single time. The second table shows for each paper if it yielded a valid result in the identification game or a failed result. ###! Here I feel like I can not summarize the tables but they take much space. Should I drop this information?

Show code 
# Any not processed papers?
paper_human_score_cld |> 
  count(`Paper ID` = paper_id) |> 
  rename(`Amount` = n) |> 
  filter(`Amount` != 1) |> 
  kable(format = "html", align = "r") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"))
Paper ID Amount
Show code 
paper_human_score_cld |>
  group_by(`Paper ID` = paper_id) |>
  summarize(
    `Valid result` = sum(is_valid),
    `Failed result`  = sum(is_failed),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(`Failed result`, `Valid result`) |>
  kable(format = "html", align = "r") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"))

Calculations

Score transformation and calculations

The total_score column contains the final TVD-MI metric. The new column hyperparameter combines the information for reasoning and non-reasoning models. The performance mean, the absolute distance to this mean and the succcess rates are calculated. For the human dataset, the success rate is by definition set to 1.

Show code 
# 1. Human Data Processing (Baseline)
paper_human_processed <- paper_human_score_cld |> 
  mutate(paper_id = as.factor(paper_id)) |> 
  mutate(across(contains("score"), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.)))) |> 
  mutate(
    total_score = (tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score - fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score),
    
    # Success rates (Metric 1); Human validity is 100% by definition
    is_valid = 1,
    is_valid_tpr = 1,
    is_valid_fpr = 1,
    
    # Performance means (Metric 2)
    group_mean = if(all(is.na(total_score))) NA_real_ else mean(total_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    tpr_mean   = if(all(is.na(tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score))) NA_real_ else mean(tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    fpr_mean   = if(all(is.na(fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score))) NA_real_ else mean(fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Individual Absolute Deviations
    abs_dev     = abs(total_score - group_mean),
    tpr_abs_dev = abs(tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score - tpr_mean),
    fpr_abs_dev = abs(fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score - fpr_mean),
    
    # Mean Absolute Deviation (Metric 3)
    mad_mean     = if(all(is.na(abs_dev))) NA_real_ else mean(abs_dev, na.rm = TRUE),
    tpr_mad_mean = if(all(is.na(tpr_abs_dev))) NA_real_ else mean(tpr_abs_dev, na.rm = TRUE),
    fpr_mad_mean = if(all(is.na(fpr_abs_dev))) NA_real_ else mean(fpr_abs_dev, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  rename_with(~ paste0(., "_human"), .cols = -c(paper_id)) |> 
  mutate(config_label_human = "Human_Global")

# 2. LLM Data Processing (Config-Level)
paper_llm_processed <- paper_llm_score_cld |> 
  mutate(paper_id = as.factor(paper_id)) |> 
  mutate(across(contains("score"), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.)))) |> 
  mutate(total_score = (tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score - fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score)) |> 
  mutate(
    model = case_match(
      model,
      "gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07" ~ "GPT-5 Mini",
      "gpt-5-nano-2025-08-07" ~ "GPT-5 Nano",
      "gpt-4o-2024-08-06" ~ "GPT-4o",
      "gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18" ~ "GPT-4o Mini",
      "o4-mini-2025-04-16" ~ "o4 Mini",
      "o3-mini-2025-01-31" ~ "o3 Mini",
      "gpt-3.5-turbo-0125" ~ "GPT-3.5 Turbo",
      "llama-3.3-70b-versatile" ~ "Llama 3.3 (70B)",
      "deepseek-r1-distill-llama-70b" ~ "DeepSeek R1 (Llama 70B)",
      .default = model
    ),
    hyperparameter = case_when(
      !is.na(reasoning_effort) ~ paste0("Re ", reasoning_effort),
      !is.na(temperature) ~ paste0("Tempr ", temperature),
      TRUE ~ "default"
    ),
    chain_of_thought = case_match(
      chain_of_thought,
      "Explain your thought process step by step." ~ "True",
      NA ~ "False",
      .default = chain_of_thought
    ),
    scope = case_match(
      scope,
      "full_text" ~ "Full text",
      "abstract"  ~ "Abstract only",
      .default = scope
    )
  ) |> 
  unite("temp_config_str", all_of(CONFIG_COL), sep = "_", remove = FALSE) |> 
  mutate(
    config_id = as.numeric(factor(temp_config_str, levels = unique(temp_config_str))),
    config_label = paste0("Config_", config_id)
  ) |> 
  dplyr::select(-temp_config_str) |> 
  group_by(config_label) |> 
  mutate(
    # Success rates (Metric 1)
    success_rate     = mean(is_valid, na.rm = TRUE),
    success_rate_tpr = mean(is_valid_tpr, na.rm = TRUE),
    success_rate_fpr = mean(is_valid_fpr, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Performance means (Metric 2)
    group_mean = if(all(is.na(total_score))) NA_real_ else mean(total_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    tpr_mean   = if(all(is.na(tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score))) NA_real_ else mean(tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    fpr_mean   = if(all(is.na(fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score))) NA_real_ else mean(fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Individual Absolute Deviations
    abs_dev     = abs(total_score - group_mean),
    tpr_abs_dev = abs(tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score - tpr_mean),
    fpr_abs_dev = abs(fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score - fpr_mean),
    
    # Mean Absolute Deviation (Metric 3)
    mad_mean     = if(all(is.na(abs_dev))) NA_real_ else mean(abs_dev, na.rm = TRUE),
    tpr_mad_mean = if(all(is.na(tpr_abs_dev))) NA_real_ else mean(tpr_abs_dev, na.rm = TRUE),
    fpr_mad_mean = if(all(is.na(fpr_abs_dev))) NA_real_ else mean(fpr_abs_dev, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  left_join(paper_human_processed |> 
              dplyr::select(
                paper_id,
                is_valid_human,
                is_valid_tpr_human,
                is_valid_fpr_human,
                total_score_human,
                group_mean_human,
                tpr_mean_human,
                fpr_mean_human,
                mad_mean_human,
                tpr_mad_mean_human,
                fpr_mad_mean_human
              ),
            by = "paper_id"
  )

# 3. Summary table
summary_table <- paper_llm_processed |> 
  group_by(config_id, model, hyperparameter, chain_of_thought, scope) |> 
  summarize(
    
    # Success
    mean_success_rate     = first(success_rate),
    mean_success_rate_tpr = first(success_rate_tpr),
    mean_success_rate_fpr = first(success_rate_fpr),
    
    # Performance (Mean)
    mean_score     = first(group_mean),
    mean_tpr_score = first(tpr_mean),
    mean_fpr_score = first(fpr_mean),
    
    # Stability (MAD)
    mean_mad     = first(mad_mean),
    mean_tpr_mad = first(tpr_mad_mean),
    mean_fpr_mad = first(fpr_mad_mean),
    
    .groups = "drop"
  )

Bootstraping

The bootstrap randomly selects 1000 values with replacing for the given metric and averages across all these values. NAs are ignored in this part. Values at the alpha level / 2 are extracted as lower and upper bounds.

Show code 
# Configuration
num_iterations <- NUM_ITERATIONS
confidence_lvl <- CONFIDENCE_LVL
global_alpha <- (1 - confidence_lvl) / 2

# Helper function
bootstrap_function <- function(df, 
                              group_col,
                              val_col,
                              tpr_val_col, 
                              fpr_val_col,
                              dev_col,
                              tpr_dev_col,
                              fpr_dev_col,
                              is_valid_col,
                              is_tpr_valid_col,
                              is_fpr_valid_col,
                              iterations = num_iterations, 
                              alpha = global_alpha) {
  
  set.seed(42)
  
  boot_distribution <- map_df(1:iterations, function(i) {
    df |> 
      group_by(.data[[group_col]]) |> 
      slice_sample(prop = 1, replace = TRUE) |> 
      summarise(
        # Plot 1: Success
        boot_success     = mean(.data[[is_valid_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        boot_success_tpr = mean(.data[[is_tpr_valid_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        boot_success_fpr = mean(.data[[is_fpr_valid_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        
        # Plot 2: Performance
        boot_mean     = mean(.data[[val_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        boot_mean_tpr = mean(.data[[tpr_val_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        boot_mean_fpr = mean(.data[[fpr_val_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        
        # Plot 3: Stability (MAD)
        boot_mad     = mean(.data[[dev_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        boot_mad_tpr = mean(.data[[tpr_dev_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        boot_mad_fpr = mean(.data[[fpr_dev_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
        
        .groups = "drop"
      )
  })
  
  # Calculate Quantiles
  ci_results <- boot_distribution |> 
    group_by(.data[[group_col]]) |> 
    summarise(
      across(starts_with("boot_"), 
             list(lower = ~quantile(.x, alpha, na.rm = TRUE),
                  upper = ~quantile(.x, 1 - alpha, na.rm = TRUE)),
             .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  return(ci_results)
}
Show code 
llm_boot_summary <- bootstrap_function(
  df               = paper_llm_processed,
  group_col        = "config_label",
  val_col          = "total_score",
  tpr_val_col      = "tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score",
  fpr_val_col      = "fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score",
  dev_col          = "abs_dev",
  tpr_dev_col      = "tpr_abs_dev",
  fpr_dev_col      = "fpr_abs_dev",
  is_valid_col     = "is_valid",
  is_tpr_valid_col = "is_valid_tpr",
  is_fpr_valid_col = "is_valid_fpr"
)

human_boot_summary <- bootstrap_function(
  df               = paper_human_processed,
  group_col        = "config_label_human",
  val_col          = "total_score_human",
  tpr_val_col      = "tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score_human",
  fpr_val_col      = "fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score_human",
  dev_col          = "abs_dev_human",
  tpr_dev_col      = "tpr_abs_dev_human",
  fpr_dev_col      = "fpr_abs_dev_human",
  is_valid_col     = "is_valid_human",
  is_tpr_valid_col = "is_valid_tpr_human",
  is_fpr_valid_col = "is_valid_fpr_human"
)
Show code 
# Join bootstrap results
paper_llm_processed_bind <- paper_llm_processed |> 
  left_join(llm_boot_summary, by = "config_label") |> 
  cross_join(
    human_boot_summary |>
      dplyr::select(-config_label_human) |> 
      rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_human")))

Plot preparation

For the specification curve plots some the configuration columns are formated.

Show code 
data_plot <- paper_llm_processed_bind |> 
  mutate(
    model = fct_relevel(
      as.factor(model),
      "DeepSeek R1 (Llama 70B)", "Llama 3.3 (70B)", "GPT-3.5 Turbo", "o3 Mini", 
      "o4 Mini", "GPT-4o Mini", "GPT-4o", "GPT-5 Nano", "GPT-5 Mini"
    ),
    hyperparameter = fct_relevel(
      as.factor(hyperparameter),
      "Tempr 1.5", "Tempr 1", "Tempr 0.5", "Tempr 0", "Re high", "Re low"
    ),
    chain_of_thought = fct_relevel(
      as.factor(chain_of_thought),
      "True", "False"
    ),
    scope = fct_relevel(
      as.factor(scope),
      "Full text", "Abstract only"
    )
  ) |> 
  rename(
    `Model` = model,
    `Hyperparameter` = hyperparameter,
    `CoT<br> used` = chain_of_thought,
    `Scope` = scope
  )

Plots

Functions

Distribution plot

Show code 
bar_function <- function(dataset, 
                         base_cols, 
                         total_col,
                         clean_labels, 
                         suffix = "", 
                         plot_title = "Distribution of Score Metrics",
                         plot_subtitle = "TPR = True positive rate; FPR = False positive rate; AVG = Average") {
  
  # Combine user-defined components with the specific total score column
  all_target_cols <- c(base_cols, total_col)
  suffixed_cols   <- paste0(all_target_cols, suffix)
  
  # Create labels: User labels for components + Automatic suffix for the total column
  final_labels <- c(
    clean_labels, 
    paste0("Total Score ", ifelse(suffix == "_human", "Human", "LLM"))
  )
  
  # Build the plot
  bar_plot <- dataset |> 
    dplyr::select(all_of(suffixed_cols)) |> 
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = everything(),
      names_to = "score_type",
      values_to = "score_value"
    ) |> 
    mutate(
      # Map the actual column names (with suffixes) to our clean labels
      score_type = factor(score_type, levels = suffixed_cols, labels = final_labels),
      # Discrete factor for the -1 to 1 scale
      score_value = factor(score_value, levels = seq(-1, 1, 0.125))
    ) |> 
    ggplot(aes(x = score_value)) +
    geom_bar(alpha = 0.8, color = "black", fill = "grey70") + 
    # scale_x_discrete ensures even empty bins are shown to represent the full range
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE, breaks = as.character(seq(-1, 1, 0.5))) +
    facet_wrap(~score_type, scales = "free_x") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = plot_title,
      subtitle = plot_subtitle,
      x = "Score Value",
      y = "Count"
    ) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "none",
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(bar_plot)
}

Specification curve plot

The plot function for a specification curve is taken from (Cummins, 2025):

Show code 
specification_curve_plot_with_shading <- function(data,
                                                  outcome = "outcome",
                                                  outcome_name = "Outcome",
                                                  plot_title = NULL,
                                                  interval_lower = NULL,
                                                  interval_upper = NULL,
                                                  rank_by = "rank",
                                                  rank_by_outcome = FALSE,
                                                  relative_height_of_upper_plot = 0.70,
                                                  outcome_cutoff = NULL,
                                                  facet_by = NULL,
                                                  facet_ncol = 2,
                                                  shade_lower = NULL,
                                                  shade_upper = NULL,
                                                  shade_fill_by = NULL,
                                                  shade_values = NULL,
                                                  shade_alpha = 0.5,
                                                  shade_fill_constant = "red",
                                                  mutate_plots = NULL) {
  require(dplyr)
  require(tidyr)
  require(ggplot2)
  require(cowplot)
  require(ggtext)
  require(rlang)
  require(grid)
  
  data <- data |>
    rename(outcome = {{outcome}}, rank = {{rank_by}})
  
  if (!is.null(interval_lower))
    data <- data |> rename(interval_lower = {{interval_lower}})
  else
    data$interval_lower <- data$outcome
  if (!is.null(interval_upper))
    data <- data |> rename(interval_upper = {{interval_upper}})
  else
    data$interval_upper <- data$outcome
  
  if (rank_by_outcome)
    data <- data |> arrange(outcome) |> mutate(rank = dplyr::row_number())
  
  keep_num <- c("rank", "outcome", "interval_lower", "interval_upper")
  if (!is.null(shade_lower))
    keep_num <- c(keep_num, as_name(ensym(shade_lower)))
  if (!is.null(shade_upper))
    keep_num <- c(keep_num, as_name(ensym(shade_upper)))
  
  data <- data |> mutate(across(.cols = -all_of(keep_num), .fns = as.factor))
  exclude_cols_base <- keep_num
  
  facet_name <- NULL
  if (!is.null(facet_by)) {
    facet_sym  <- ensym(facet_by)
    facet_name <- as_name(facet_sym)
    if (!facet_name %in% names(data))
      stop("facet_by column not found in data.")
  }
  
  # specify column that controls fill (will be facet)
  if (!is.null(shade_fill_by)) {
    fill_name <- as_name(ensym(shade_fill_by))
  } else {
    fill_name <- facet_name
  }
  if (!is.null(fill_name))
    exclude_cols_base <- c(exclude_cols_base, fill_name)
  
  # resolve global fill levels & colors (so colors are consistent across facets)
  fill_levels <- if (!is.null(fill_name))
    levels(droplevels(as.factor(data[[fill_name]])))
  else
    NULL
  resolved_fill <- NULL
  if (!is.null(fill_levels) && !is.null(shade_values)) {
    if (!is.null(names(shade_values)) &&
        any(nzchar(names(shade_values)))) {
      resolved_fill <- shade_values[fill_levels]
    } else {
      resolved_fill <- rep(shade_values, length.out = length(fill_levels))
      names(resolved_fill) <- fill_levels
    }
  }
  
  build_one <- function(df,
                        title_text = plot_title,
                        show_y_titles = TRUE) {
    
    # REFINEMENT: Ensure rank order for smooth ribbon rendering
    df <- df |> arrange(rank)
    
    p_estimates <- df |>
      mutate(outcome_name = outcome_name) |>
      ggplot(aes(rank, outcome))
    
    # shaded band behind points/intervals
    if (!is.null(shade_lower) && !is.null(shade_upper)) {
      lower_sym <- ensym(shade_lower)
      upper_sym <- ensym(shade_upper)
      
      band <- df |>
        summarise(
          ymin = dplyr::first(!!lower_sym),
          ymax = dplyr::first(!!upper_sym),
          .groups = "drop"
        )
      
      if (!is.null(fill_name) && fill_name %in% names(df)) {
        band$.__fill <- dplyr::first(df[[fill_name]])
        p_estimates <- p_estimates +
          geom_rect(
            data = band,
            aes(
              xmin = -Inf,
              xmax = Inf,
              ymin = ymin,
              ymax = ymax,
              fill = .__fill
            ),
            alpha = shade_alpha,
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            show.legend = FALSE
          )
        if (!is.null(resolved_fill)) {
          p_estimates <- p_estimates + scale_fill_manual(values = resolved_fill, guide = "none")
        }
      } else {
        p_estimates <- p_estimates +
          geom_rect(
            data = band,
            aes(
              xmin = -Inf,
              xmax = Inf,
              ymin = ymin,
              ymax = ymax
            ),
            fill = shade_fill_constant,
            alpha = shade_alpha,
            inherit.aes = FALSE,
            show.legend = FALSE
          )
      }
      # ensure band is within the y-scale
      p_estimates <- p_estimates + expand_limits(y = c(band$ymin, band$ymax))
    }
    
    # REFINEMENT: Shaded Ribbon CI instead of linerange
    p_estimates <- p_estimates +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = interval_lower, ymax = interval_upper), 
                  fill = "#ADD8E6", alpha = 0.4) +
      geom_point(shape = "circle", size = 2) +
      facet_grid(
        outcome_name ~ .,
        space = "free_y",
        scales = "free_y",
        switch = "y"
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks()) +
      scale_x_continuous(NULL, expand = c(.02, .02)) +
      ylab(if (show_y_titles)
        "Simulation results"
        else
          NULL) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        legend.position   = "none",
        axis.line.x       = element_blank(),
        strip.placement   = "outside",
        strip.background  = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),
        panel.spacing.x   = unit(0.15, "cm"),
        strip.text.y.left = if (show_y_titles)
          ggtext::element_markdown(
            angle = 90,
            face = "bold",
            size = 7,
            margin = margin(r = 20, l = 5) # REFINEMENT
          )
        else
          element_blank(),
        axis.text.y       = element_text(
          angle = 0,
          hjust = 0.5,
          size = 6
        ),
        axis.title.y      = if (show_y_titles)
          element_text(
            size = 9,
            face = "bold",
            margin = margin(r = 4)
          )
        else
          element_blank(),
        panel.spacing     = unit(0.25, "lines"),
        axis.title.x      = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x       = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x      = element_blank()
      )
    
    if (!is.null(outcome_cutoff)) {
      p_estimates <- p_estimates + geom_hline(yintercept = outcome_cutoff, linetype = "dotted")
    }
    
    exclude_cols <- exclude_cols_base
    if (!is.null(facet_name))
      exclude_cols <- c(exclude_cols, facet_name)
    
    p_specs <- df |>
      tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -dplyr::all_of(exclude_cols)) |>
      arrange(rank) |>
      ggplot(aes(
        x = rank,
        y = factor(value),
        color = name
      )) +
      geom_point(size = 3.5, shape = "|") +
      facet_grid(name ~ .,
                 space = "free_y",
                 scales = "free_y",
                 switch = "y") +
      guides(color = "none") +
      scale_x_continuous(NULL, expand = c(.02, .02)) +
      ylab(if (show_y_titles)
        "Simulation conditions"
        else
          NULL) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        strip.placement   = "outside",
        strip.background  = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = NA),
        panel.spacing.x   = unit(0.15, "cm"),
        strip.text.y.left = if (show_y_titles)
          ggtext::element_markdown(
            angle = 90,
            face = "bold",
            size = 7,
            # REFINEMENT:
            lineheight = 0.9,
            margin = margin(r = 20, l = 5)
          )
        else
          element_blank(),
        axis.text.y       = element_text(
          angle = 0,
          hjust = 1,
          size = 6
        ),
        axis.title.y      = if (show_y_titles)
          element_text(
            size = 9,
            face = "bold",
            margin = margin(r = 4)
          )
        else
          element_blank(),
        panel.spacing     = unit(0.25, "lines"),
        axis.title.x      = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x       = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x      = element_blank()
      ) +
      scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2")
    
    if (is.function(mutate_plots)) {
      out <- mutate_plots(p_estimates, p_specs, df)
      if (inherits(out, "gg")) {
        p_estimates <- out
      } else if (is.list(out)) {
        if (length(out) >= 1)
          p_estimates <- out[[1]]
        if (length(out) >= 2)
          p_specs     <- out[[2]]
      }
    }
    
    g_est <- ggplotGrob(p_estimates)
    g_spc <- ggplotGrob(p_specs)
    max_w <- unit.pmax(g_est$widths, g_spc$widths)
    g_est$widths <- max_w
    g_spc$widths <- max_w
    
    combo <- cowplot::plot_grid(
      g_est,
      g_spc,
      axis = "bltr",
      align = "v",
      ncol = 1,
      rel_heights = c(relative_height_of_upper_plot, 1)
    )
    if (!is.null(title_text)) {
      title_grob <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
        cowplot::draw_label(
          title_text,
          x = 0.5,
          hjust = 0.5,
          y = 0.5, # REFINEMENT
          vjust = 0.5, # REFINEMENT
          fontface = "bold"
        )
      combo <- cowplot::plot_grid(title_grob,
                                  combo,
                                  ncol = 1,
                                  rel_heights = c(0.15, 1)) # REFINEMENT
    }
    combo
  }
  
  if (is.null(facet_by))
    return(build_one(data, plot_title, show_y_titles = TRUE))
  
  facet_vals <- if (is.factor(data[[facet_name]]))
    levels(droplevels(data[[facet_name]]))
  else
    unique(data[[facet_name]])
  plotlist <- lapply(seq_along(facet_vals), function(i) {
    v  <- facet_vals[i]
    df <- dplyr::filter(data, .data[[facet_name]] == v)
    if (nrow(df) == 0)
      return(NULL)
    local_title <- if (!is.null(plot_title))
      paste0(plot_title, ": ", v)
    else
      paste0(facet_name, ": ", v)
    is_left_col <- ((i - 1) %% facet_ncol) == 0
    build_one(df, local_title, show_y_titles = is_left_col)
  })
  plotlist <- Filter(Negate(is.null), plotlist)
  
  cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = plotlist,
                     ncol = facet_ncol,
                     align = "hv")
}

Distribution llm scores

Show code 
paper_llm_scores <- bar_function(
  dataset      = paper_llm_processed,
  base_cols    = BASE_SCORE_COLS,
  total_col    = TOTAL_SCORE_COL,
  clean_labels = CLEAN_SCORE_COLS
)

paper_llm_scores

Distribution human scores

Show code 
paper_human_scores <- bar_function(
  dataset      = paper_human_processed,
  base_cols    = BASE_SCORE_COLS,
  total_col    = TOTAL_SCORE_COL,
  clean_labels = CLEAN_SCORE_COLS,
  suffix = "_human",
)

paper_human_scores

Success rate

Show code 
###! My idea is to place all the tpr/fpr plots beside the main TVD-MI score plots or to put them in the appendix; In case I keep all nine plots, should I wrapp all of this into a function?

success_spec_plot <- data_plot |>  
  # Preprocess
  dplyr::select(
    `Model`, 
    `Hyperparameter`, 
    `CoT<br> used`, 
    `Scope`, 
    success_rate,
    boot_success_lower,      
    boot_success_upper
  ) |>  
  # Summarize
  group_by(Model, Hyperparameter, `CoT<br> used`, Scope) |>
  summarise(
    success_rate = mean(success_rate),
    boot_success_lower = mean(boot_success_lower),
    boot_success_upper = mean(boot_success_upper),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  arrange(success_rate) |>
  mutate(rank = row_number()) |> 
  # Plot call
  specification_curve_plot_with_shading(
  outcome = success_rate,
  outcome_name = "Success rate TVD-MI",
  plot_title = "Specification Curve: Success rate by Configuration",
  interval_lower = "boot_success_lower",
  interval_upper = "boot_success_upper",
  rank_by = rank,
  
  mutate_plots = \(top, bottom, df) {
    list(top + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)))
  }
)
Show code 
success_tpr_spec_plot <- data_plot |>  
  # Preprocess
  dplyr::select(
    `Model`, 
    `Hyperparameter`, 
    `CoT<br> used`, 
    `Scope`, 
    success_rate_tpr,
    boot_success_tpr_lower,      
    boot_success_tpr_upper
  ) |>  
  # Summarize
  group_by(Model, Hyperparameter, `CoT<br> used`, Scope) |>
  summarise(
    success_rate_tpr = mean(success_rate_tpr),
    boot_success_tpr_lower = mean(boot_success_tpr_lower),
    boot_success_tpr_upper = mean(boot_success_tpr_upper),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  arrange(success_rate_tpr) |>
  mutate(rank = row_number()) |> 
  # Plot call
  specification_curve_plot_with_shading(
  outcome = success_rate_tpr,
  outcome_name = "Success rate TPR",
  plot_title = "Specification Curve: Success rate by Configuration",
  interval_lower = "boot_success_tpr_lower",
  interval_upper = "boot_success_tpr_upper",
  rank_by = rank,
  
  mutate_plots = \(top, bottom, df) {
    list(top + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)))
  }
)

Mean

Show code 
###! The quality assessment ignores how many unsuccessful combos a configuration had. Should I include this information, e.g. via the transparency of the data point (alpha) or might this more confusing than helpful?

mean_spec_plot <- data_plot |> 
  # Preprocess
  dplyr::select(
    `Model`, 
    `Hyperparameter`, 
    `CoT<br> used`, 
    `Scope`, 
    group_mean, 
    boot_mean_lower, 
    boot_mean_upper,
    boot_mean_lower_human, 
    boot_mean_upper_human
  ) |> 
  # Summarize
  group_by(Model, Hyperparameter, `CoT<br> used`, Scope) |> 
  summarize(
    group_mean = mean(group_mean),
    boot_mean_lower = mean(boot_mean_lower),
    boot_mean_upper = mean(boot_mean_upper),
    boot_mean_lower_human = mean(boot_mean_lower_human),
    boot_mean_upper_human = mean(boot_mean_upper_human),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  arrange(group_mean) |>
  mutate(rank = row_number()) |> 
  # Plot call
  specification_curve_plot_with_shading(
    # Basic content
    outcome = group_mean,
    outcome_name = "Mean TVD-MI score",
    plot_title = "Specification Curve: Mean by Configuration",
    interval_lower = "boot_mean_lower",
    interval_upper = "boot_mean_upper",
    rank_by = rank,
    # CI base
    shade_lower = "boot_mean_lower_human",
    shade_upper = "boot_mean_upper_human",
    
    mutate_plots = \(top, bottom, df) {
      list(top + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 1)))
    }
  )

distance_spec_plot <- data_plot |>  
  # Preprocess
  dplyr::select(
    `Model`, 
    `Hyperparameter`, 
    `CoT<br> used`, 
    `Scope`, 
    mad_mean, 
    boot_mad_lower, 
    boot_mad_upper,
    boot_mad_lower_human,
    boot_mad_upper_human
  ) |> 
  # Summarize
  group_by(Model, Hyperparameter, `CoT<br> used`, Scope) |> 
  summarize(
    mad_mean = mean(mad_mean),
    boot_mad_lower = mean(boot_mad_lower),
    boot_mad_upper = mean(boot_mad_upper),
    boot_mad_lower_human = mean(boot_mad_lower_human),
    boot_mad_upper_human = mean(boot_mad_upper_human),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  arrange(mad_mean) |>
  mutate(rank = row_number()) |> 
  # Plot call

    # Basic content
    outcome = mad_mean,
    outcome_name = "Avg. dis. to<br>mean TVD-MI score",
    plot_title = "Specification Curve: Average distance by Configuration",
    interval_lower = "boot_mad_lower",
    interval_upper = "boot_mad_upper",
    rank_by = rank,
    # CI base
    shade_lower = "boot_mad_lower_human",
    shade_upper = "boot_mad_upper_human",
    
    mutate_plots = \(top, bottom, df) {
      list(top + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 1)))
    }
  )

Inferential tests

Show code 
# Function for conditional posthoc tests
conditional_posthoc_function <- function(model, alpha = 0.05, adjust = "bonferroni") {
  
  # Check if model is binomial (glmer) or linear (lmer)
  is_glmer <- inherits(model, "glmerMod")
  test_stat <- if(is_glmer) "Chisq" else "F"
  
  anova_res <- tryCatch({
    car::Anova(model, type = "II", test.statistic = test_stat)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("ANOVA failed: ", e$message)
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  if(is.null(anova_res)) return(NULL)
  
  # Identify the p-value column (it's either Pr(>Chisq) or Pr(>F))
  p_col <- grep("Pr\\(>", colnames(anova_res), value = TRUE)
  
  # Handle cases where no terms are significant
  sig_mask <- anova_res[[p_col]] < alpha
  terms <- rownames(anova_res)[sig_mask]
  terms <- terms[!terms %in% c("(Intercept)", "Residuals")]
  
  if(length(terms) == 0) {
    message("No significant terms found at alpha ", alpha)
    return(list(anova = anova_res, posthoc = list()))
  }
  
  # Run post-hoc tests
  results <- lapply(terms, function(term) {

    spec_formula <- as.formula(paste("~", term))
    
    # Calculate Estimated Marginal Means
    emm <- emmeans::emmeans(model, specs = spec_formula, type = "response")
    
    # Compact Letter Display (CLD)
    multcomp::cld(emm, 
                  alpha = alpha, 
                  Letters = c(letters, LETTERS), 
                  adjust = adjust, 
                  reversed = TRUE) |> 
      as.data.frame() |> 
      mutate(term = term)
  })
  
  names(results) <- terms
  return(list(anova = anova_res, posthoc = results))
}

Success rates

Show code 
# 1. Total TVD-MI Success
glmer_success_total <- lme4::glmer(is_valid ~ model + hyperparameter + chain_of_thought + scope + (1 | paper_id),
                                   data = paper_llm_processed, family = binomial)
posthoc_success_total <- conditional_posthoc_function(glmer_success_total, alpha = ALPHA, adjust = POST_HOC_CORRECTION)

# 2. TPR Success
glmer_success_tpr <- lme4::glmer(is_valid_tpr ~ model + hyperparameter + chain_of_thought + scope + (1 | paper_id),
                                 data = paper_llm_processed, family = binomial)
posthoc_success_tpr <- conditional_posthoc_function(glmer_success_tpr, alpha = ALPHA, adjust = POST_HOC_CORRECTION)

# 3. FPR Success
glmer_success_fpr <- lme4::glmer(is_valid_fpr ~ model + hyperparameter + chain_of_thought + scope + (1 | paper_id),
                                 data = paper_llm_processed, family = binomial)
posthoc_success_fpr <- conditional_posthoc_function(glmer_success_fpr, alpha = ALPHA, adjust = POST_HOC_CORRECTION)
Show code 
posthoc_success_total
$anova

hyperparameter   4.4781  4     0.3452
chain_of_thought 0.0002  1     0.9900
scope            2.0638  1     0.1508

$posthoc
list()

chain_of_thought 0.1659  1     0.6838
scope            0.0428  1     0.8361

hyperparameter   3.0415  4     0.5509
chain_of_thought 0.9022  1     0.3422
scope            0.1678  1     0.6821

list()

Mean performance

Show code 
###! By default the function deletes any combo which includes NAs. This can lead to bias, benefitial for combos with a small amount of kept rows, as my data is not missing at random. Can I still run them like this?

# 4. Total Score Mean
lmm_mean_total <- lme4::lmer(total_score ~ model + hyperparameter + chain_of_thought + scope + (1 | paper_id),
                             data = paper_llm_processed)
posthoc_mean_total <- conditional_posthoc_function(lmm_mean_total, alpha = ALPHA, adjust = POST_HOC_CORRECTION)

# 5. TPR Score Mean
lmm_mean_tpr <- lme4::lmer(tpr_avg_tvd_mi_score ~ model + hyperparameter + chain_of_thought + scope + (1 | paper_id),
                           data = paper_llm_processed)
posthoc_mean_tpr <- conditional_posthoc_function(lmm_mean_tpr, alpha = ALPHA, adjust = POST_HOC_CORRECTION)

# 6. FPR Score Mean
lmm_mean_fpr <- lme4::lmer(fpr_avg_tvd_mi_score ~ model + hyperparameter + chain_of_thought + scope + (1 | paper_id),
                           data = paper_llm_processed)
posthoc_mean_fpr <- conditional_posthoc_function(lmm_mean_fpr, alpha = ALPHA, adjust = POST_HOC_CORRECTION)
Show code 
posthoc_mean_total
$anova

scope            0.4535  1 2119.5 0.5008

$posthoc

Stability (Mean Absolute Deviation)

Show code 
# 7. Total Score Stability (MAD)
lmm_mad_total <- lme4::lmer(abs_dev ~ model + hyperparameter + chain_of_thought + scope + (1 | paper_id),
                            data = paper_llm_processed)
posthoc_mad_total <- conditional_posthoc_function(lmm_mad_total, alpha = ALPHA, adjust = POST_HOC_CORRECTION)

# 8. TPR Stability (MAD)
lmm_mad_tpr <- lme4::lmer(tpr_abs_dev ~ model + hyperparameter + chain_of_thought + scope + (1 | paper_id),
                          data = paper_llm_processed)
posthoc_mad_tpr <- conditional_posthoc_function(lmm_mad_tpr, alpha = ALPHA, adjust = POST_HOC_CORRECTION)

# 9. FPR Stability (MAD)
lmm_mad_fpr <- lme4::lmer(fpr_abs_dev ~ model + hyperparameter + chain_of_thought + scope + (1 | paper_id),
                          data = paper_llm_processed)
posthoc_mad_fpr <- conditional_posthoc_function(lmm_mad_fpr, alpha = ALPHA, adjust = POST_HOC_CORRECTION)
